metadata.dataset_name: RBI KYC Master Direction — Golden Evaluation Dataset

schema.topic: Topic area within the KYC Master Direction
schema.type: Factual | Eligibility | Implementation | Comparison | Edge Case | Equity | Multi Hop | Unanswerable
schema.question: User query to send to the RAG chatbot
schema.expected_answer_detailed: Full expected answer for LLM-as-judge evaluation
schema.evaluation_criteria: What a correct chatbot response must contain
schema.source_pages: Page numbers in the RBI KYC Master Direction
schema.query_variations: Natural paraphrases for retrieval robustness testing
schema.requires_reasoning: True = keyword match alone cannot answer correctly
schema.expected_chunks: Number of document sections needed for a complete answer
schema.hallucination_risk: Low | Medium | High — risk of chatbot fabricating an answer

golden_dataset.question: What are the four key elements that every KYC policy must include?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: Customer Acceptance Policy, Risk Management, Customer Identification Procedures (CIP), and Monitoring of Transactions.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: As per paragraph 5 of Chapter II, the KYC policy shall include four key elements: (a) Customer Acceptance Policy; (b) Risk Management; (c) Customer Identification Procedures (CIP); and (d) Monitoring of Transactions.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must name all four elements correctly. Missing any one = incomplete answer.
query_variations: What must a bank's KYC policy contain?
List the components of RBI KYC policy
What does the KYC master direction require in a KYC policy?

golden_dataset.question: Which documents are considered Officially Valid Documents (OVDs) under the KYC Master Direction?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: Passport, driving licence, Aadhaar proof of possession, Voter ID, NREGA job card signed by state officer, and NPR letter with name and address.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: As per paragraph 3(a)(xiv), Officially Valid Documents (OVDs) are: passport, driving licence, proof of possession of Aadhaar number, Voter's Identity Card issued by Election Commission of India, job card issued by NREGA duly signed by an officer of the State Government, and letter issued by the National Population Register containing details of name and address.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must list all six OVDs. Must not add unlisted documents. PAN card is NOT an OVD by itself.
query_variations: What ID documents does RBI accept for KYC?
Which documents can be used as proof of identity for bank account opening?
What is an OVD as per RBI KYC rules?

golden_dataset.question: What are the transaction limits for a Small Account?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: Annual credits ≤ ₹1 lakh; monthly withdrawals/transfers ≤ ₹10,000; balance at any time ≤ ₹50,000.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: As per paragraph 23, a Small Account has the following limitations: (i) aggregate of all credits in a financial year does not exceed ₹1 lakh; (ii) aggregate of all withdrawals and transfers in a month does not exceed ₹10,000; and (iii) balance at any point of time does not exceed ₹50,000. Note: government grants and welfare benefit deposits are excluded from the balance limit.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must state all three limits with correct figures: ₹1 lakh annual credit, ₹10,000 monthly withdrawal, ₹50,000 balance.
query_variations: How much money can be kept in a small account?
What are the limits on deposits and withdrawals in RBI small accounts?
Can I deposit more than 1 lakh in a small account?

golden_dataset.question: For how many years must a bank maintain transaction records under the KYC Master Direction?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: At least 5 years from date of transaction; identification records must be kept 5 years after business relationship ends.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: As per paragraph 46, REs shall: (a) maintain all necessary records of transactions for at least five years from the date of transaction; (b) preserve records pertaining to customer identification and addresses for at least five years after the business relationship is ended.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must state 5 years for both categories. Must distinguish that transaction records are from date of transaction, while identification records run from end of relationship.
query_variations: How long should KYC documents be kept by banks?
What is the record retention period for AML compliance in India?
How many years do banks keep transaction data under PML Rules?

golden_dataset.question: Can the Principal Officer be appointed as the Designated Director?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: No. The Principal Officer shall not be nominated as the Designated Director.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: No. As per paragraph 6(d), in no case shall the Principal Officer be nominated as the 'Designated Director'. Both are separate roles.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must clearly say NO. Must mention the explicit prohibition in paragraph 6(d).
query_variations: Can the same person be both Designated Director and Principal Officer?
Is it allowed to combine the roles of Principal Officer and Designated Director?
What restriction exists on appointing the Designated Director in banks?

golden_dataset.question: Within how many days must a Regulated Entity upload KYC records to the Central KYC Records Registry (CKYCR)?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: Within 10 days of commencement of an account-based relationship.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: As per paragraph 56(b), REs shall capture customer's KYC records and upload onto CKYCR within 10 days of commencement of an account-based relationship. Updated KYC information must be furnished within 7 days.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must state 10 days for initial upload. Bonus: mentioning 7 days for subsequent updates.
query_variations: How soon must banks upload KYC data to CERSAI?
What is the deadline for uploading KYC records to CKYCR?
When must a bank submit KYC to the central registry?

golden_dataset.question: How frequently must KYC be updated for high-risk, medium-risk, and low-risk customers?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: High-risk: every 2 years. Medium-risk: every 8 years. Low-risk: every 10 years.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: As per paragraph 38, periodic KYC updation must be carried out at least: once in every two years for high-risk customers; once in every eight years for medium-risk customers; once in every ten years for low-risk customers.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must correctly state all three periodicities: 2 years (high), 8 years (medium), 10 years (low). Wrong figures = wrong answer.
query_variations: How often does a bank customer need to update KYC?
What is the KYC renewal frequency for high risk customers in India?
Does an RBI KYC update expire? How often?

golden_dataset.question: What originator information must accompany all cross-border wire transfers?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: Name, account number, address/national ID/customer ID/date-place of birth, beneficiary name, and beneficiary account number.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: As per paragraph 64(A)(i), all cross-border wire transfers must be accompanied by: (a) name of the originator; (b) originator's account number; (c) originator's address, or national identity number, or customer identification number, or date and place of birth; (d) name of the beneficiary; and (e) beneficiary's account number.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must list all five data points including beneficiary information.
query_variations: What details must be sent with an international wire transfer?
What information is required for cross-border remittances under RBI rules?
What data must accompany a foreign wire transfer from India?

golden_dataset.question: Under what conditions can a bank refuse to open an account?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: If unable to apply CDD due to customer non-cooperation or unreliable documents; anonymous/benami names; or no transaction without CDD.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: Under paragraph 10, REs may refuse account opening in these cases: (a) anonymous or fictitious/benami names; (b) when unable to apply appropriate CDD measures due to non-cooperation of the customer or non-reliability of documents. Reasons for rejection must be recorded.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must state the two valid grounds for refusal. Must mention that reason must be documented. Should note that banks cannot reject just because a customer is poor or disadvantaged.
query_variations: Can a bank deny opening an account to someone?
When is a bank allowed to refuse a customer under KYC rules?
What are valid reasons for a bank to reject an account opening?

golden_dataset.question: For which types of customers and purposes can Video-based Customer Identification Process (V-CIP) be used?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: New individual customers, proprietors, authorised signatories and BOs of legal entities; also for OTP-KYC account conversion and periodic KYC updation.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: As per paragraph 18, V-CIP can be used for: (i) CDD of new customers — individuals, proprietors, authorised signatories and Beneficial Owners; (ii) conversion of existing accounts opened using Aadhaar OTP-based e-KYC; and (iii) updation/periodic updation of KYC. V-CIP is treated on par with face-to-face CIP.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must mention all three use cases. Must note that V-CIP is equivalent to face-to-face CIP.
query_variations: Who can use V-CIP for account opening?
Can a company open a bank account using video KYC?
Is video-based KYC accepted for periodic updation?

golden_dataset.question: Can a bank open an account for a Politically Exposed Person (PEP)? What conditions apply?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: Yes, but requires senior management approval, source of funds verification, enhanced ongoing monitoring, and appropriate risk management systems.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: Yes. As per paragraph 41, REs may open PEP accounts provided: (a) appropriate risk management systems exist; (b) reasonable measures to establish source of funds/wealth; (c) senior management approval is obtained; (d) enhanced monitoring on an ongoing basis. These requirements also apply to family members and close associates of PEPs.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must say YES (not prohibited). Must list all four conditions. Must mention applicability to family members and associates.
query_variations: Are banks allowed to serve politicians?
What are the rules for opening accounts for government officials in India?
Is enhanced due diligence required for PEPs in Indian banks?

golden_dataset.question: What Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD) measures must be followed for non-face-to-face customer onboarding (outside V-CIP)?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: V-CIP offered first; no alternate mobile number post-CDD; positive address confirmation before operations; PAN verified; first transaction must be credit from existing KYC-compliant account; categorised as high-risk.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: As per paragraph 40: (a) V-CIP shall be offered as the first option; (b) alternate mobile numbers shall not be linked post-CDD; (c) current address must be verified by positive confirmation; (d) PAN must be obtained and verified; (e) first transaction must be a credit from an existing KYC-complied bank account; (f) such customers shall be categorised as high-risk.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must mention at least 4 of the 6 EDD measures. High-risk categorisation and V-CIP as first option are mandatory to mention.
query_variations: What extra checks are needed for online account opening in India?
What is enhanced due diligence for digital onboarding?
How should banks handle customers who never came to a branch?

golden_dataset.question: Can a bank restrict account operations merely because it has filed a Suspicious Transaction Report (STR)?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: No. Banks shall not put any restriction on operations in accounts merely on the basis of an STR filed.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: No. As per paragraph 49, REs shall not put any restriction on operations in the accounts merely on the basis of an STR filed. Filing an STR is a confidential obligation — the fact of filing must not be disclosed to the customer (tipping-off prohibition).
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must clearly say NO to restricting operations based on STR alone. Must mention the tipping-off prohibition as the rationale.
query_variations: Can a bank freeze an account just because it filed an STR?
Does filing a suspicious transaction report allow blocking a customer's account?
What happens to a customer's account after an STR is filed?

golden_dataset.question: What AML/CFT obligations must Indian banks meet before establishing a correspondent banking relationship?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: Board-approved policy; gather info on respondent bank's business/reputation/AML controls; senior management prior approval; document AML responsibilities; no shell bank relationships.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: As per paragraph 63, before and during a correspondent banking relationship, banks must: (a) have a Board-approved policy; (b) gather sufficient information about the respondent bank — business, management, reputation, AML/CFT controls; (c) obtain prior approval from senior management; (d) clearly document AML/CFT responsibilities; (e) never enter into or continue relationships with shell banks; (f) be cautious with institutions in jurisdictions with FATF deficiencies.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must mention: Board-approved policy, prohibition on shell banks, senior management approval, and verification of respondent bank's AML controls.
query_variations: What due diligence is required for correspondent bank relationships?
Can Indian banks have accounts with shell banks abroad?
What does RBI require before establishing a nostro/vostro relationship?

golden_dataset.question: What are the restrictions on accounts opened using Aadhaar OTP-based e-KYC in non-face-to-face mode?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: Deposit balance ≤ ₹1 lakh; annual credits ≤ ₹2 lakh; term loans only ≤ ₹60,000/year; valid for 1 year only; cannot be used with another RE simultaneously.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: As per paragraph 17: (i) aggregate balance of all deposit accounts ≤ ₹1 lakh; (ii) aggregate credits in a financial year ≤ ₹2 lakh; (iii) only term loans sanctioned ≤ ₹60,000 per year; (iv) accounts valid for maximum one year unless full CDD is completed; (v) customer declaration that no other OTP-KYC account opened at any other RE.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must correctly state all numeric limits: ₹1L balance, ₹2L annual credit, ₹60K term loan, 1-year validity. Must not confuse with small account limits.
query_variations: What is the maximum deposit in an OTP KYC account?
How long can a non-face-to-face Aadhaar OTP account remain active?
What loans can be given to OTP e-KYC account holders?

golden_dataset.question: How often must a Regulated Entity conduct a Money Laundering and Terrorist Financing (ML/TF) Risk Assessment?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: Periodically as determined by the Board, but must be reviewed at least annually.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: As per paragraph 5A, REs shall carry out ML/TF Risk Assessment periodically. The periodicity shall be determined by the Board, but must be reviewed at least annually. The assessment must be documented and placed before the Board.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must say 'at least annually' as the minimum frequency. Must mention Board oversight and documentation requirement.
query_variations: How often should a bank review its AML risk assessment?
What is the minimum frequency for ML/TF risk assessment under RBI rules?
Does an Indian bank need to do annual money laundering risk reviews?

golden_dataset.question: What is the difference between Customer Due Diligence (CDD) and Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD)?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: CDD is standard identity verification and risk profiling; EDD is additional scrutiny applied to high-risk customers with extra measures like source of funds verification and senior management approval.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: CDD (paragraph 3(b)(v)) means identifying and verifying the customer and beneficial owner using reliable, independent identification sources. EDD goes beyond CDD and applies to high-risk situations such as: non-face-to-face customers (paragraph 40), PEPs (paragraph 41), customers from FATF-deficient jurisdictions (paragraph 54).
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must correctly define both CDD and EDD. Must give at least two examples of when EDD applies. Must note EDD is additional to, not a replacement of, CDD.
query_variations: What extra steps does EDD involve compared to normal KYC?
When does a bank need enhanced due diligence?
How is EDD different from standard customer verification?

golden_dataset.topic: Small Account vs OTP e-KYC Account
golden_dataset.question: Compare the deposit balance limits between a Small Account and an OTP-based e-KYC account.
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: Small Account: balance ≤ ₹50,000; OTP e-KYC account: aggregate balance across all accounts ≤ ₹1 lakh.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: Small Account (paragraph 23): maximum balance ₹50,000; annual credits ≤ ₹1 lakh; monthly withdrawals ≤ ₹10,000. OTP e-KYC Account (paragraph 17): aggregate balance ≤ ₹1 lakh; annual credits ≤ ₹2 lakh; term loans only ≤ ₹60,000/year. The OTP e-KYC account has a higher balance ceiling.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must correctly state ₹50,000 for small account balance and ₹1 lakh for OTP e-KYC balance. Must not swap figures between the two account types.
query_variations: Which account type allows higher balance — small account or OTP KYC account?
Is there a difference between small account limits and Aadhaar OTP account limits?
Compare balance restrictions for simplified KYC accounts in India

golden_dataset.question: What are the different responsibilities of Ordering RE and Beneficiary RE in wire transfers?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: Ordering RE ensures complete originator/beneficiary info and verifies identity if structuring suspected. Beneficiary RE verifies received information and may reject transfers missing required information.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: Under paragraph 64(B): Ordering RE must ensure all wire transfers contain required and accurate originator and beneficiary information; must not execute wire transfers if unable to comply. The Beneficiary RE receives the wire transfer and makes funds available; it must verify received information is complete.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must correctly distinguish Ordering RE (initiates, verifies originator info) from Beneficiary RE (receives, makes funds available). Must not conflate their roles.
query_variations: What does the sending bank have to do in a wire transfer under RBI rules?
Who is responsible for originator information in a wire transfer?
What are the AML duties of the bank receiving an international wire?

golden_dataset.question: What should a bank do if it suspects money laundering but believes that completing the CDD process will tip off the customer?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: Stop the CDD process and file an STR with FIU-IND instead. Do not proceed with CDD.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: As per paragraph 11A, where a RE forms a suspicion of money laundering or terrorist financing and reasonably believes that performing the CDD process will tip off the customer, it shall not pursue the CDD process and shall instead file a Suspicious Transaction Report (STR) with FIU-IND.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must state: (1) stop CDD, (2) file STR with FIU-IND. Must not say the bank should complete CDD first. Tipping-off prevention rationale should be mentioned.
query_variations: What does a bank do if completing KYC would warn a money launderer?
Can a bank skip CDD if it suspects AML and would tip the customer off?
What is the tipping-off rule in Indian banking?
golden_dataset.tags: tipping-off, STR, FIU-IND, edge-case, AML, Chapter-III

golden_dataset.question: What is the beneficial ownership threshold for a company versus an unincorporated association under the KYC Master Direction?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: Company: >10% controlling ownership interest. Unincorporated association: >15% ownership of property/capital/profits.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: As per paragraph 3(a)(iv): For a company, the beneficial owner is the natural person with more than 10% of the shares, capital, or profits. For an unincorporated association or body of individuals: more than 15% of property, capital, or profits. Where no natural person meets the threshold, the senior managing official is treated as the beneficial owner.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must correctly state 10% for companies/partnerships and 15% for unincorporated associations. Mixing up these thresholds is a clear error.
query_variations: At what shareholding does someone become a beneficial owner of a company for KYC?
What is the UBO threshold for trusts and associations in India?
How is beneficial ownership defined differently for companies vs societies under RBI rules?

golden_dataset.question: What is the consequence for a bank if it fails to file an STR for a money mule account that it has identified?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: It shall be deemed that the bank has not complied with the KYC/AML Master Direction.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: As per paragraph 59, if it is established that an account opened and operated is that of a Money Mule, but no STR was filed by the concerned bank, it shall then be deemed that the bank has not complied with these directions. Banks must undertake meticulous monitoring to identify money mule accounts and report suspicious transactions to FIU-IND.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must state the specific consequence: non-compliance with the Master Direction. Must mention FIU-IND reporting obligation.
query_variations: What happens if a bank ignores a money mule account?
Is failure to report a money mule a violation in India?
What penalty does an Indian bank face for missing an STR on a mule account?

golden_dataset.question: Does the KYC Master Direction allow banks to deny banking services to financially or socially disadvantaged persons?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: No. The Customer Acceptance Policy must not result in denial of banking services to socially/financially disadvantaged persons including Persons with Disabilities.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: No. As per paragraph 11, the Customer Acceptance Policy shall not result in denial of banking/financial facility to members of the general public, especially those who are financially or socially disadvantaged, including Persons with Disabilities (PwDs). Reason(s) for any rejection must be duly recorded.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must clearly say NO. Must mention PwDs specifically. Must note that rejection reasons must be recorded.
query_variations: Can poor people be denied bank accounts due to KYC rules?
What does RBI say about banking access for disabled persons?
Is financial exclusion allowed under the KYC Master Direction?

golden_dataset.question: Does a bank need to complete CDD for all members of a Self Help Group (SHG) when opening a savings account?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: No. CDD of all office bearers suffices. CDD of all members may be done at the time of credit linking.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: No. As per paragraph 43: (a) CDD of all members of the SHG shall not be required while opening the savings bank account; (b) CDD of all the office bearers shall suffice; (c) CDD of all members may be undertaken at the time of credit linking of SHGs.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must confirm that full member CDD is NOT required at account opening. Must mention that office bearer CDD suffices and member CDD happens at credit linking.
query_variations: Do all SHG members need KYC for account opening?
What are the simplified KYC rules for self help groups in India?
Can a SHG open an account without full KYC for every member?

golden_dataset.question: How does the KYC Master Direction define a Shell Bank, and what restrictions exist on correspondent banking relationships with shell banks?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: Shell bank = no physical presence (meaningful mind and management) in country of incorporation, unaffiliated with regulated group. Banks must never enter or continue correspondent relationships with shell banks.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: Definition (paragraph 3(b)(xv)): A Shell Bank is a bank that has no physical presence in the country in which it is incorporated and licensed, and is unaffiliated with a regulated financial group. Physical presence means meaningful mind and management. Restrictions (paragraph 63(g) and (h)): Correspondent banking relationships shall not be entered into or continued with a shell bank. Banks must also ensure respondent banks do not permit their accounts to be used by shell banks.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must correctly define shell bank using the 'meaningful mind and management' language. Must state the absolute prohibition on correspondent banking with shell banks.
query_variations: What is a shell bank under RBI rules?
Can Indian banks do business with shell banks?
How does RBI define physical presence for a bank?

golden_dataset.question: What sanctions lists must Indian banks check, and how frequently must they be verified?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: UNSCR 1267/1989/2253 (Al-Qaida), UNSCR 1988 (Taliban), UNSCR 1718 (DPRK), WMD Act designated list, UAPA schedules — all must be checked daily.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: Under Chapter IX, banks must verify daily: (1) ISIL/Al-Qaida Sanctions List under UNSCR 1267/1989/2253; (2) Taliban Sanctions List under UNSCR 1988; (3) UNSCR 1718 Sanctions List for DPRK; (4) WMD Act 2005 designated list maintained by FIU-India; (5) Lists in the first and fourth schedules of UAPA, 1967. Modifications must be taken into account on the day they are made.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must name at least 3 of the 5 sanctions lists. Must state 'daily' verification frequency.
query_variations: How often must Indian banks screen customer names against sanctions lists?
Which UN sanctions lists are Indian banks required to check?
What is the UAPA obligation for banks regarding terrorist accounts?
golden_dataset.tags: sanctions, UNSCR, UAPA, WMD, multi-hop, Chapter-IX, daily-verification

golden_dataset.question: If a customer is re-categorised from medium-risk to high-risk during a periodic review, how does this affect their KYC updation schedule?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: Their updation frequency changes from once in 8 years (medium) to once in 2 years (high).
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: Chapter IV (paragraph 12) requires periodic review of risk categorisation at least once in six months. Chapter VI (paragraph 38) sets updation frequencies: high-risk = once in 2 years; medium-risk = once in 8 years. If a customer is re-categorised from medium to high-risk, their KYC updation must thereafter occur at least once every 2 years.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must correctly state the before (8 years) and after (2 years) frequencies. Must note that periodic risk review happens at least every 6 months.
query_variations: If my risk level is upgraded by my bank, how soon do I need to update KYC?
What happens to KYC schedule when a customer moves from medium to high risk?
How often is customer risk category reviewed under RBI KYC rules?
golden_dataset.tags: multi-hop, risk-categorisation, periodic-updation, high-risk, Chapter-IV, Chapter-VI

golden_dataset.question: If a fully KYC-compliant customer wants to open a second account at the same bank, do they need to complete KYC again? What about if they go to a different bank?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: Same bank: no fresh CDD needed. Different bank: can use KYC Identifier from CKYCR — no re-submission unless info has changed or records are incomplete.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: Same bank: As per paragraph 10(f) and paragraph 26, KYC verified at one branch/office is valid for transfer to any other branch/office, and no fresh CDD exercise is needed. Different bank: As per paragraph 56(j), the new RE shall seek the KYC Identifier from CKYCR and shall not require the customer to resubmit KYC records unless: (i) information has changed; (ii) KYC record is incomplete; (iii) document validity has lapsed; or (iv) the RE needs to verify identity/address.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must correctly state: no fresh CDD at same bank; CKYCR/KYC Identifier route for different bank with four exceptions listed.
query_variations: Do I need to redo KYC if I open another account at the same bank?
Can I use my existing KYC at a new bank?
What is the KYC identifier and how does it help customers switching banks?
golden_dataset.tags: multi-hop, KYC-transfer, CKYCR, KYC-identifier, same-bank, Chapter-VI, Chapter-X

golden_dataset.question: What is the monetary penalty for violating the RBI KYC Master Direction?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: Not specified in this document.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: The KYC Master Direction does not specify monetary penalties for violations. Penalty amounts are governed by other legislation such as the PML Act, 2002 or the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, which are separate documents not covered in this Master Direction.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must say penalties are not specified in this document. Must not hallucinate a figure.
query_variations: How much fine can RBI impose for KYC non-compliance?
What is the penalty for AML violations in Indian banks?
Is there a specific fine for not filing an STR?

golden_dataset.question: Does the RBI KYC Master Direction cover KYC requirements for cryptocurrency or virtual digital asset transactions?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: Not covered in this document. Virtual digital assets are not mentioned.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: The KYC Master Direction does not cover cryptocurrency or virtual digital assets. KYC requirements for virtual digital asset service providers are governed by separate RBI/SEBI/government guidelines not included in this Master Direction.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must state VDA/crypto is not covered. Must not fabricate rules.
query_variations: Do I need KYC for crypto in India?
What does RBI say about KYC for Bitcoin transactions?
Are virtual assets covered under the AML KYC rules in India?

golden_dataset.question: What is the exact number of days within which an STR must be filed after detecting a suspicious transaction?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: The specific timeline is not stated in this document. Paragraph 49 references the PML Rules but does not restate the deadline.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: The KYC Master Direction does not specify the exact number of days within which an STR must be filed. Paragraph 49 states that 'delay of each day in not reporting a transaction beyond the time limit as specified in the Rule shall be constituted as a separate violation', but refers to the PML (Maintenance of Records) Rules, 2005, which is a separate document.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must acknowledge the document references a deadline but does not state it. Must not fabricate a specific number of days.
query_variations: Within how many days must an STR be filed in India?
What is the STR filing deadline under RBI KYC rules?
How quickly must suspicious transactions be reported to FIU-IND?

golden_dataset.question: Which specific countries are classified as high-risk jurisdictions by FATF, as per this KYC Master Direction?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: Not listed in this document. The direction says to refer to FATF Statements circulated by RBI from time to time.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: The KYC Master Direction does not list specific high-risk countries or jurisdictions. Paragraph 54 states that REs shall consider 'FATF Statements circulated by Reserve Bank of India from time to time'. The specific list is dynamic and externally sourced from FATF, not embedded in this document.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must NOT name specific countries. Must explain the document refers to external, dynamic FATF lists. Naming specific countries would be a hallucination.
query_variations: Which countries are blacklisted for banking in India?
What are the FATF grey list countries under RBI rules?
Does RBI list high-risk countries for banking?

golden_dataset.question: Does the RBI KYC Master Direction mandate the use of Artificial Intelligence for transaction monitoring?
golden_dataset.reference_answer_short: No — it is encouraged, not mandated. Paragraph 36 says REs 'may consider' AI/ML for ongoing due diligence.
golden_dataset.expected_answer_detailed: No, AI/ML use is not mandated by the KYC Master Direction. Paragraph 36 states that 'for ongoing due diligence, REs may consider adopting appropriate innovations including artificial intelligence and machine learning (AI & ML) technologies to support effective monitoring.' The word 'may' makes this optional encouragement, not a mandatory requirement.
golden_dataset.evaluation_criteria: Must correctly identify 'may consider' as permissive not mandatory. Must not say AI is required.
query_variations: Must Indian banks use AI for AML transaction monitoring?
Is machine learning required for suspicious transaction detection under RBI rules?
Does RBI make AI mandatory for KYC compliance?
